scope: Asia-based family-law practitioners (advocates / solicitors / barristers / bengoshi) in India, Singapore, Hong Kong, Japan who have appeared as named counsel in apex or reported PA-relevant litigation, published in the field at scholarly / book-length / firm thought-leadership level, sit on an official PA-related expert / committee body, or publicly stated a sourced stance through institutional or NGO advocacy.

stance_taxonomy.recognition: Treats parental alienation / 子の連れ去り / 共同親権 reform as a legitimate, litigable phenomenon
stance_taxonomy.critique: Argues PA / SAP is misused in family courts, especially against DV survivor mothers; supports proscription or strong DV carve-outs
stance_taxonomy.middle: Procedural-reform / differentiated approach engaging both DV-protection and alienating-behaviour frames
stance_taxonomy.not_publicly_confirmed: Included for record-completeness; PA-specific stance not in primary sources

practitioners.name: Geeta Luthra
practitioners.credentials: Senior Advocate, Supreme Court of India (designated 2007); LLM + MPhil Cambridge
practitioners.firm: Geeta Luthra & Associates / independent senior counsel SCI
practitioners.address: Chamber 47, Supreme Court of India, New Delhi
practitioners.bar: Bar Council of India / Supreme Court of India
named_cases_or_publications: Lt Col Vivek Singh v Romani Singh, Civil Appeal 3962/2016, (2017) 3 SCC 231 (counsel for respondent mother)
Multiple inter-country custody habeas-corpus matters
IAFL India fellow profile
practitioners.hook: Senior Advocate who actually argued and won India's most-cited Supreme Court PA judgment; the single highest-leverage Indian apex-court interlocutor for the recognition camp.

practitioners.name: Anil Malhotra

Justice Rajesh Bindal Committee Report to Government of India 2018
practitioners.hook: India's most internationally networked PA / international-child-removal practitioner; IAFL Fellow + Bindal Committee member with the only Indian practitioner monograph series specifically on removed-children law.

practitioners.name: Ranjit Malhotra

One of three India special invitees, Hague Experts' Meeting University of Westminster 12 June 2017
practitioners.hook: Anil's brother and the firm's English-courts-facing expert-witness practitioner; natural second name on any India-side Hague / abduction / PA collaboration.
